name: Scallops & Cashews in Tangerine Sauce
recipeIngredient: Grated tangerine peel, 2 tsp (or finely minced)
recipeInstructions:
This recipe can be adapted beautifully to be vegetarian! I love this dish with cubed tofu instead of scallops, and if you can't find a vegetarian oyster sauce, just substitute a bit of soy sauce or experiment with your favorite Asian sauce.
Advance Preparation: Place the nuts and oil in a small saucepan over medium-high heat. Stir the nuts occasionally until they turn very light golden, then drain immediately and pat dry on paper towels. Reserve 2 tablespoons of the cooking oil for stir-frying. Set aside green onions, garlic, and scallops in separate containers.
Combine sauce ingredients in a small bowl and mix well.
Last-Minute Cooking: Mix cornstarch with an equal amount of cold water. Set aside.
Place a wok over highest heat until very hot. Add reserved oil to the center, swirling it around the sides of the wok. Add garlic when it just begins to turn white (about 15 seconds). Add scallops and stir-fry until they just lose their raw outer color (about 1 minute). Stir in green onions and pour in the sauce. When the sauce comes to a low boil, stir in a little of the cornstarch mixture so that the sauce glazes the scallops. Stir in cashews, then taste and adjust seasonings. Spoon onto a heated platter or individual plates and serve immediately.
